refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.

candidate_dps: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes
Periodic full model refresh at fixed intervals
Event-driven update triggered by external signals or sensor inputs
Hybrid approach combining incremental updates with periodic full refreshes
Prioritized update focusing on critical model elements first
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes

coupling_check.notes: The incremental update DP isolates update logic to only changed parts of the model, minimizing dependencies and supporting independence from other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Incremental updates can be reliably detected and applied within the latency target
Model consistency is maintained after partial updates
External events triggering updates are timely and accurate
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining update triggering mechanisms and fallback strategies to ensure robustness under varying operational conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing model updates and integration with other system components.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data inputs and sends updated model state information.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates incremental updates and synchronization signals with external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental model update mechanism is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as updates are managed internally with limited external exposure.
Partial containment possible but model update logic is not primarily vehicle-specific, leading to significant diffusion across other modules.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and tasking, not on model update mechanisms, causing poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services are concerned with alerts and safety monitoring, not model updates, resulting in very weak containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental model update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, as it centralizes model management and minimizes cross-module dependencies.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface design to avoid unintended diffusion.
If model updates require vehicle-specific adaptations, some diffusion into Vehicle module may occur.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes' directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness. The DP logically satisfies the functional requirement by focusing on incremental updates to meet latency constraints.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may cause unintended diffusion if interface design is not carefully managed.
Possible diffusion risk into Vehicle module if vehicle-specific adaptations are needed for model updates.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating good architectural packaging of the DP. Other modules show weaker containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) and ensuring operational policies are enforced within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The IM boundary 'NGHE Solution' clearly encompasses the DP and its interfaces, providing a well-defined ownership and implementation scope for the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts with Telemetry and Network Link to minimize diffusion risks.
Assess and document any vehicle-specific adaptations required to handle potential diffusion into the Vehicle module.
Include verification steps in the NGHE Solution module to measure update latency and compliance with operational policies.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical satisfaction of the FR by the DP, good architectural containment and low diffusion in the IM, and feasible verification of the FR through the IM. The recommended revisions are documentation and verification steps rather than fundamental decomposition needs. Given the high safety criticality, the current level is sufficiently detailed to proceed with implementation and verification without further sub-FR decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface contracts with Telemetry and Network Link modules and assess vehicle-specific adaptations to minimize diffusion risks, and to include verification steps measuring update latency and policy compliance within the NGHE Solution module.

assumptions: The incremental model update DP fully satisfies the timeliness and correctness requirements of the FR.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate ownership boundary for the DP implementation.
Verification metrics (p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s) are sufficient to confirm FR satisfaction.
open_risks: Potential diffusion risks with Telemetry, Network Link, and Vehicle modules if interface contracts are not well defined.
Vehicle-specific adaptations may introduce complexity or diffusion if not properly managed.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts and adaptation assessments.
Verification results demonstrating update latency and policy enforcement within NGHE Solution module.
